page: Microsoft Azure M64s is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the MS family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8280M processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It includes 2097 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, but does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by poor ratings in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core tests. However, the server demonstrates strong multi-threaded capabilities, particularly in large-block read-only memory bandwidth and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Most multi-core operations, including Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Redis SET operations, perform in the average tier.

Given its massive 1 TB memory capacity and average multi-core processing capabilities, the M64s is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that require high resource density rather than peak single-thread compute speeds. The server is well-suited for large-scale database operations, memory-cached web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks, while being less optimal for single-threaded applications or heavy LLM text generation.
description: The Microsoft Azure M64s is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical Intel Xeon 8280M cores running at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM and 2097 GB of local storage, this Hyper-V-powered instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, with poor results in single-threaded compression and Geekbench single-core tests. Conversely, the server excels in multi-threaded operations, showing strong large-block read-only memory bandwidth and strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and Redis SET throughput remain in the average tier. This hardware profile makes the M64s qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads, such as large databases and multi-threaded data processing, where massive RAM capacity is prioritized over high single-threaded compute speeds.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64s is a memory-optimized server with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 2097 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-threaded memory bandwidth but weak single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64s is a memory-optimized server with 64 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 2097 GB local storage for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring one terabyte of RAM and sixty-four dedicated vCPUs for memory-intensive multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8280M at 2.7 GHz
1024.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core
2097 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-threaded memory bandwidth and bzip3 decompression performance
Weak single-core performance in compression and single-threaded benchmarks
Best suited for large-scale databases and memory-intensive multi-threaded workloads
